info.title: FastAPI
info.description: AIDE Gateway — proxy surface + Temporal job surface.

post.description: Parse a document (multipart/form-data) and return the parse response inline. Returns 504 if parsing does not finish within the wait window — use the async ``POST /jobs`` route for long-running documents.

schema.description: The parse result: ``markdown``, ``structure``, per-element ``grounding``, and ``metadata`` (``req_id``, ``job_id``, ``model_version``, ``page_count``, ``markdown_chars``, ``failed_pages``, ``duration_ms``, and a ``billing`` block with ``service_tier`` and ``total_credits``). The resolved model version is echoed in ``metadata.model_version``.

206.description: Partial success — some pages failed to parse

schema.description: The parse result: ``markdown``, ``structure``, per-element ``grounding``, and ``metadata`` (``req_id``, ``job_id``, ``model_version``, ``page_count``, ``markdown_chars``, ``failed_pages``, ``duration_ms``, and a ``billing`` block with ``service_tier`` and ``total_credits``). The resolved model version is echoed in ``metadata.model_version``.

document.description: The document to parse (PDF or image, ≤ 50 MiB). Exactly one of ``document`` / ``document_url``.

document_url.description: Public http(s) URL to fetch the document from. Exactly one of ``document`` / ``document_url``.

received_at.description: Epoch seconds the job was received.

created_at.description: Epoch seconds the job was created.

data.description: Inline result, set once completed (unless delivered to ``output_save_url``).

output_url.description: Where the result was delivered instead of inline ``data``.

post.description: Parse a document asynchronously: returns 202 with the job envelope; poll ``GET /jobs/{job_id}``.

document.description: The document to parse (PDF or image, ≤ 50 MiB). Exactly one of ``document`` / ``document_url``.

document_url.description: Public http(s) URL to fetch the document from. Exactly one of ``document`` / ``document_url``.

output_save_url.description: Public URL the full response is delivered to; the API response then carries ``output_url`` instead of inline data.

priority.description: Async execution tier (``POST /jobs`` only). ``priority`` runs in the fast lane at the sync billing rate.

get.description: Poll a parse job. ``data`` (or ``output_url`` when delivery was requested) is set once completed.

received_at.description: Epoch seconds the job was received.

created_at.description: Epoch seconds the job was created.

data.description: Inline result, set once completed (unless delivered to ``output_save_url``).

output_url.description: Where the result was delivered instead of inline ``data``.

post.description:
Run synchronously and return the result inline.

Accepts ``application/json`` (the request fields as the body) or
``multipart/form-data`` (file fields are staged automatically).

Returns **504** if the work does not finish within the wait
window — long-running work belongs on the async ``POST
{path}/jobs`` route, which returns 202 immediately and is polled
via ``GET {path}/jobs/{job_id}``. On a 504 the workflow is
CANCELLED (a sync caller can never collect the result); a retry
with the same ``idempotency_key`` starts the work over.

schema.description:
Result returned by V2ExtractOperationWorkflow — the ``/v2/extract``
response body (``docs/extract-v2-proposal.md`` → Response).

``extraction`` and ``extraction_metadata`` mirror each other structurally:
leaf values in ``extraction`` are replaced by ``ExtractionFieldMetadata``
objects in ``extraction_metadata``.

extraction.description: Extracted values conforming to the request ``schema``.
extraction.title: Extraction

extraction_metadata.description: Per-field metadata, mirroring ``extraction`` with leaf values replaced by ``{value, spans}`` objects.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

metadata.description: Request metadata (job_id, version, duration_ms, doc_id, credit_usage).

schema.title: V2ExtractResult

schema.description:
Input to V2ExtractOperationWorkflow.

Provide the markdown inline (small inputs) or as a data-store ref from
``POST /v1/files`` (large inputs). Exactly one of ``markdown``,
``markdown_ref``, or ``markdown_url`` must be set.
JSON Schema describing the fields to extract. The schema must be an object type with a ``properties`` map of field names to their types and descriptions.
schema.title: Schema

markdown.description: Markdown string to extract from, or a multipart FILE part carrying the markdown (uploads are staged and handled as ``markdown_ref``). Can come from any source — LandingAI parse output, a third-party parser, or hand-authored text. When the markdown was produced by ``POST /v2/parse``, it ends with a ``<!-- doc_id=<id> -->`` comment that the service reads automatically and echoes as ``metadata.doc_id``.
markdown.title: Markdown

markdown_ref.description: Data-store ref for large markdown inputs. Upload via ``POST /v1/files`` → pass the returned ``file_ref`` here, or send the file directly as a multipart form part under this name.
markdown_ref.title: Markdown Ref

markdown_url.description: URL to fetch the markdown from. Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time.
markdown_url.title: Markdown Url

idempotency_key.description: Optional client key; a retried request with the same key attaches to the already-running job instead of starting a duplicate.

schema.title: V2ExtractRequest

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the fields to extract. The schema must be an object type with a ``properties`` map of field names to their types and descriptions. JSON-serialized string in form data.
schema.title: Schema

anyOf.description: Markdown string to extract from, or a multipart FILE part carrying the markdown (uploads are staged and handled as ``markdown_ref``). Can come from any source — LandingAI parse output, a third-party parser, or hand-authored text. When the markdown was produced by ``POST /v2/parse``, it ends with a ``<!-- doc_id=<id> -->`` comment that the service reads automatically and echoes as ``metadata.doc_id``.
Data-store ref for large markdown inputs. Upload via ``POST /v1/files`` → pass the returned ``file_ref`` here, or send the file directly as a multipart form part under this name.

markdown_url.description: URL to fetch the markdown from. Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time. JSON-serialized string in form data.
markdown_url.title: Markdown Url

model.description: Model override, e.g. ``extract-20260630`` / ``extract-latest``. JSON-serialized string in form data.
model.title: Model

options.description: Extraction options (``strict``). Omit for defaults. JSON-serialized string in form data.

idempotency_key.description: Optional client key; a retried request with the same key attaches to the already-running job instead of starting a duplicate.

get.description:
Poll a job.

Returns ``{job_id, status, created_at}`` plus ``completed_at`` and
``result`` (on ``completed``) or ``error: {code, message}`` (on
``failed``). Statuses: ``pending`` → ``processing`` →
``completed`` | ``failed``.

completed_at.description: Present once the job is terminal.

progress.description: Best-effort progress, present while processing only for jobs that report it.

extraction.description: Extracted values conforming to the request ``schema``.
extraction.title: Extraction

extraction_metadata.description: Per-field metadata, mirroring ``extraction`` with leaf values replaced by ``{value, spans}`` objects.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

metadata.description: Request metadata (job_id, version, duration_ms, doc_id, credit_usage).

post.description:
Run synchronously and return the result inline.

Accepts ``application/json`` (the request fields as the body) or
``multipart/form-data`` (file fields are staged automatically).

Returns **504** if the work does not finish within the wait
window — long-running work belongs on the async ``POST
{path}/jobs`` route, which returns 202 immediately and is polled
via ``GET {path}/jobs/{job_id}``. On a 504 the workflow is
CANCELLED (a sync caller can never collect the result); a retry
with the same ``idempotency_key`` starts the work over.

schema.description:
Result returned by V2WorkflowOperationWorkflow.

``output`` is keyed by step name (or by caller-chosen projection keys when
``output`` was provided in the request). For the ``"parse-extract"`` pipeline
with no projection, the keys are ``"parse-extract"`` → ``{parse, extract, ground}``:

* ``output["parse-extract"]["parse"]`` — full parse response (markdown,
  structure, grounding, metadata).
* ``output["parse-extract"]["extract"]`` — extraction + ``extraction_metadata``
  with ``{value, spans}`` leaves.
* ``output["parse-extract"]["ground"]`` — a tree MIRRORING
  ``extraction_metadata``: each ``{value, spans}`` leaf is replaced by its
  resolved ``[{page, box}]`` list (or ``null`` for a synthesised value). Nested
  objects and arrays keep their shape, so ``ground.issuer.name`` parallels
  ``extraction_metadata.issuer.name``. Resolved server-side — no client-side
  span lookup needed.

References into the results use the ``$output.<step>.<field>...`` grammar
(see ``output`` field of the request).

output.description: Step results keyed by step name, or a caller-defined projection. For ``parse-extract`` with no projection: ``{"parse-extract": {parse, extract, ground}}``.
output.title: Output

schema.title: V2WorkflowResult

schema.description:
Input to V2WorkflowOperationWorkflow.

Phase 1 API design (``docs/pipeline-v2-proposal.md``):

``inputs`` declares named document sources. Each entry binds a logical name
to a binary upload (via ``document``, the multipart field name) or a URL
(via ``document_url``). After the gateway pre-processor runs, each input
carries a resolved ``document_ref`` (storage-ref) or ``document_url``.

``steps`` is a one-element array containing a prebuilt step. Its ``document``
field references an entry from ``inputs`` (``"$inputs.<name>"``). The ``schema``
field carries the JSON Schema for the extract stage.

``output`` is an optional projection map. Each key maps to a ``$output.``
reference into the step results. Omitted → the full results tree is returned
under ``output``.

Example — minimal request (parse-extract, default options)::

    inputs = {"report": {"document": "report_pdf"}}
    steps  = [{"name": "parse-extract", "document": "$inputs.report",
               "schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"revenue": {"type": "string"}}}}]
    report_pdf = [binary]

inputs.description: Named document sources. Each key is a logical name referenced from steps as ``"$inputs.<name>"``. The gateway pre-processor resolves binary multipart parts to ``document_ref`` before validation.
inputs.title: Inputs

steps.description: Phase 1: a single prebuilt-pipeline step (name must be ``"parse-extract"``). Its result appears in ``output`` under the step's name.
steps.title: Steps

output.description: Optional projection map. Keys are caller-chosen response field names; values are ``$output.<step>.<field>...`` references. Omitted → full results tree returned under ``output``.
output.title: Output

idempotency_key.description: Optional client key; a retried request with the same key attaches to the already-running job instead of starting a duplicate.

schema.title: V2WorkflowRequest

inputs.description: Named document sources. Each key is a logical name referenced from steps as ``"$inputs.<name>"``. The gateway pre-processor resolves binary multipart parts to ``document_ref`` before validation. JSON-serialized string in form data.
inputs.title: Inputs

steps.description: Phase 1: a single prebuilt-pipeline step (name must be ``"parse-extract"``). Its result appears in ``output`` under the step's name. JSON-serialized string in form data.
steps.title: Steps

output.description: Optional projection map. Keys are caller-chosen response field names; values are ``$output.<step>.<field>...`` references. Omitted → full results tree returned under ``output``. JSON-serialized string in form data.
output.title: Output

idempotency_key.description: Optional client key; a retried request with the same key attaches to the already-running job instead of starting a duplicate.

    inputs = {"report": {"document": "report_pdf"}}
    steps  = [{"name": "parse-extract", "document": "$inputs.report",

idempotency_key.description: Optional client key; a retried request with the same key attaches to the already-running job instead of starting a duplicate.

priority.description: Async execution tier. ``priority`` runs in the fast lane at the sync billing rate; absent → ``standard``.

schema.title: V2WorkflowRequest

inputs.description: Named document sources. Each key is a logical name referenced from steps as ``"$inputs.<name>"``. The gateway pre-processor resolves binary multipart parts to ``document_ref`` before validation. JSON-serialized string in form data.
inputs.title: Inputs

steps.description: Phase 1: a single prebuilt-pipeline step (name must be ``"parse-extract"``). Its result appears in ``output`` under the step's name. JSON-serialized string in form data.
steps.title: Steps

output.description: Optional projection map. Keys are caller-chosen response field names; values are ``$output.<step>.<field>...`` references. Omitted → full results tree returned under ``output``. JSON-serialized string in form data.
output.title: Output

priority.description: Async execution tier. ``priority`` runs in the fast lane at the sync billing rate; absent → ``standard``.

get.description:
Poll a job.

Returns ``{job_id, status, created_at}`` plus ``completed_at`` and
``result`` (on ``completed``) or ``error: {code, message}`` (on
``failed``). Statuses: ``pending`` → ``processing`` →
``completed`` | ``failed``.

completed_at.description: Present once the job is terminal.

progress.description: Best-effort progress, present while processing only for jobs that report it.

output.description: Step results keyed by step name, or a caller-defined projection. For ``parse-extract`` with no projection: ``{"parse-extract": {parse, extract, ground}}``.
output.title: Output

post.description:
Stage bytes on the data plane; pass the returned ``file_ref`` in
job inputs (the contract request types take ``*_ref`` fields).

schema.title: Response Stage File V1 Files Post

strict.description: When ``true``, returns HTTP 422 if the schema contains fields the model cannot extract. When ``false`` (default), unsupported fields are skipped and extraction continues.
strict.title: Strict

V2ExtractMetadata.description: Response metadata for a v2 extract call.

job_id.description: Gateway job id (workflow id). Matches the ``x-request-id`` the gateway minted for this request and the billing row id in vision-agent.
job_id.title: Job Id

doc_id.description: Present when the input markdown contained a ``<!-- doc_id=<id> -->`` comment (embedded by ``POST /v2/parse``). Links this extract call to the originating parse job.
doc_id.title: Doc Id

V2ExtractMetadata.title: V2ExtractMetadata

PrebuiltWorkflowStep.description:
A Phase 1 step: references a server-defined pipeline by name.

The ``name`` identifies the prebuilt pipeline to run AND becomes the key
under which this step's result appears in the response ``output`` map.
The ``document`` field is a reference into the ``inputs`` namespace
(``"$inputs.<logical-name>"``).

name.description: Workflow to run — doubles as the result key in ``output``. v1 only: ``"parse-extract"``.
name.title: Name

document.description: Reference into the ``inputs`` namespace, e.g. ``"$inputs.report"``. The referenced input must be declared in the ``inputs`` form field.
document.title: Document

schema.description: JSON Schema for fields to extract.
schema.title: Schema

options.description: Parse + extract options for this step. Omit for defaults.

PrebuiltWorkflowStep.title: PrebuiltWorkflowStep

WorkflowDocumentInput.description:
One declared document input in the ``inputs`` map.

Exactly one of ``document``, ``document_ref``, or ``document_url`` must be set:

* ``document`` — name of a multipart form part carrying the raw bytes.
  The gateway pre-processor stages those bytes to the data store and replaces
  this field with the resulting ``document_ref`` before TypeAdapter validation.
* ``document_ref`` — data-store ref from a prior ``POST /v1/files`` call.
* ``document_url`` — public URL (SSRF-validated at submit; fetched and staged
  to the data store by the gateway before the workflow starts).

document.description: Name of the multipart form part carrying the document bytes. The gateway resolves this to ``document_ref`` before validation; it is not stored in the final request object.
document.title: Document

document_ref.description: Data-store ref (resolved from binary upload by the gateway).
document_ref.title: Document Ref

document_url.description: URL to fetch the document from. Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time.
document_url.title: Document Url

WorkflowDocumentInput.title: WorkflowDocumentInput

WorkflowStepOptions.description:
Unified options for the ``parse-extract`` prebuilt step.

``pages`` applies to the parse stage; ``strict`` applies to the extract
stage. Omit entirely to use each stage's defaults.

pages.description: 0-indexed page indices to process. ``null`` = all pages. Negative indices are rejected; indices ≥ page_count are silently ignored.
pages.title: Pages

strict.description: When ``true``, returns 422 if the schema contains fields the model cannot extract. When ``false``, unsupported fields are skipped.
strict.title: Strict

WorkflowStepOptions.title: WorkflowStepOptions

V2WorkflowMetadata.description:
Response metadata for a v2 workflow call
(``docs/pipeline-v2-proposal.md`` → Top-level metadata).

duration_ms.description: Total end-to-end duration across all stages in milliseconds.
duration_ms.title: Duration Ms

credit_usage.description: Combined credits billed for this request (parse + extract).
credit_usage.title: Credit Usage

V2WorkflowMetadata.title: V2WorkflowMetadata
